instruction: open settings of terminal in android studio

location.label: Settings gear icon in Terminal panel header
location.name: Settings gear icon in Terminal panel header
location.reasoning: The gear icon is visible in the top-right of the Terminal panel header. Candidate z0 correctly identifies the settings gear icon, which when clicked will open the Terminal settings dialog in Android Studio.

history.target_visible_element: Android Studio window with Terminal panel and settings
history.reasoning: Stage 1: Crop to the Android Studio application window which contains the Terminal panel at the bottom. The Terminal tab is visible along with other tabs (TODO, Problems, Services). Within this window, we can find the gear/settings icon for the terminal or navigate to File > Settings > Tools > Terminal. This crop keeps all relevant context for finding the terminal settings in subsequent rounds.

history.target_visible_element: Terminal panel header with settings gear icon
history.reasoning: Need to zoom into the Terminal panel area at the bottom of Android Studio. The Terminal tab shows 'Local' and there should be a settings gear icon on the right side of the terminal panel header. Cropping to the terminal panel header region to locate the settings control for the terminal.

history.target_visible_element: settings gear icon in terminal panel header
history.reasoning: The current crop shows the Terminal panel header with the bash dropdown open showing a 'Settings' option, and a settings gear icon visible on the far right of the header. The gear icon is the standard, always-visible control for opening terminal settings in Android Studio/IntelliJ. Cropping to the right portion of the terminal header to focus on the gear icon and adjacent window control buttons (the local control group), while excluding the dropdown area which is a transient menu state.
